name: Estuary Flow Data Pipeline API
what_it_does: Real-time data pipeline platform with REST API for managing capture (CDC from databases), transformation, and materialization flows — using a declarative spec model to move data between 200+ sources and destinations with sub-second latency.
use_cases: Setting up CDC pipelines from PostgreSQL, MySQL to data warehouses via API
Managing real-time data flows between 200+ connectors programmatically
Monitoring pipeline health and throughput metrics for data reliability agents
Deploying declarative Flow specifications for ETL workflows as code
Streaming database changes to analytics systems with exactly-once semantics
not_for: Simple batch ETL without streaming requirements (use Airbyte or Fivetran)
Message queuing without data transformation (use Kafka directly)
Self-hosted only environments without cloud connectivity
best_when: An agent manages real-time data pipelines with CDC requirements, needing sub-second latency and declarative pipeline management across many connectors.
avoid_when: You need simple batch ELT with no streaming requirements — Fivetran or Airbyte are simpler.

auth.notes: JWT bearer tokens from Estuary dashboard. Tokens scoped to specific catalog namespaces. Service account tokens for automation. Token refresh required — tokens have expiry.

pricing.notes: Generous free tier for development and small pipelines. Pay-as-you-go pricing model. More affordable than Fivetran for high-volume CDC. Open-source Flow runtime with cloud managed service option.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not publicly documented; pipeline throughput scales with plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Good developer documentation with OpenAPI spec. Declarative spec model well-documented. Error responses include catalog validation errors and pipeline state information. Pipeline state transitions require polling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Declarative spec upserts are idempotent — applying same spec is safe. Pipeline activation is idempotent. Data processing uses exactly-once semantics.
known_agent_gotchas: Declarative spec model has learning curve — agents must understand catalog namespace structure
Pipeline activation is async — poll task state until active before querying throughput
CDC source connectors require database credentials with replication permissions
Connector compatibility matrix varies — verify source/destination support before building

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. Namespace-scoped bearer tokens. TLS enforced. Connector secrets encrypted at rest. Open-source runtime allows security audit of data plane. EU data residency available.
